summary: Validated record with machine-checkable proof for the CrashControl DisplayParameters setting. The app writes the documented value and Microsoft Support documents the 0/1 semantics.

setting.name: Display Blue Screen Parameters
setting.casual_explanation: This tweak tries to show extra technical parameters on a blue screen. That can help advanced debugging, but most users will not benefit from the extra numbers.
setting.why_people_change_it: People enable it when they are actively investigating a crash and want more detail on the blue screen itself.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Showing more crash data can help debugging, and the registry semantics are now validated against a primary Microsoft support article.
setting.professional_notes: The app writes HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\CrashControl\DisplayParameters = 1. Microsoft Support documents the setting on the CrashControl surface and explicitly states that 0 does not display the stop error information while 1 does display it.

allowed_values.label: Do not display stop error information
allowed_values.meaning: Microsoft Support documents this CrashControl value as the state that hides the extra stop-error details from the blue screen.

allowed_values.label: Display stop error information
allowed_values.meaning: Microsoft Support documents this CrashControl value as the state that shows additional stop-error details on the blue screen.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisplayParameters = 1 under CrashControl to surface more crash detail on the blue screen.

windows_defaults.label: Windows published default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Systems where DisplayParameters has not been formally mapped in this dataset

states.rationale: The Microsoft Support article documents 0 as the state that does not display stop error information.

windows_defaults.notes: The Microsoft Support article documents the registry semantics directly, so the record no longer needs to stay conservative.

recommended_profiles.label: Hold for research

states.rationale: Use the documented state that displays stop error information.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to show stop error details for debugging.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need a different crash-screen behavior.
tradeoffs: The app behavior is documented honestly.
The setting is now published from a primary Microsoft support article.

recommended_profiles.label: Current app profile

states.rationale: Observed app value that is still waiting for a primary-source mapping.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are documenting the current app behavior with a validated mapping.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need a different crash-screen behavior.
tradeoffs: The observed tweak behavior is captured.
The value semantics are now published as officially validated.

evidence.title: Microsoft Support: Stop error information isn't displayed on the blue screen in Windows
evidence.summary: Microsoft Support documents HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\CrashControl\DisplayParameters with explicit 0 and 1 meanings. 0 does not display stop error information; 1 displays it.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisplayParameters = 1 under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\CrashControl for the current tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft Support documents DisplayParameters directly on CrashControl with explicit 0/1 semantics, and the app writes the documented display state.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\CrashControl; "0 - Do not display the Stop error information"; "1 - Display the Stop error information".
validation_proof.notes: Validated against Microsoft Support documentation for the CrashControl DisplayParameters value.
